title: FlowState
description: A real-time collaborative workspace for remote design teams with live cursors and version history.
overview: FlowState is a collaborative design workspace that lets distributed teams edit Figma-like canvases in real time. Built for a Series A startup, it replaced a patchwork of Slack threads and static exports with a single source of truth for design reviews.
challenge: The client needed sub-100ms cursor sync across 20+ concurrent users, offline resilience for flaky connections, and a permissions model that matched their existing RBAC system. Legacy tools couldn't handle the scale without expensive enterprise licenses.
solution: I architected a WebSocket layer on Node.js with operational transformation for conflict resolution, paired with a React canvas built on Konva. Redis pub/sub handles horizontal scaling, while IndexedDB caches enable offline edits that sync on reconnect. A custom RBAC middleware maps roles to canvas-level permissions.
results: Reduced design review cycles from 3 days to 4 hours. Supports 50 concurrent users with p99 latency under 80ms. Adopted by 12 teams within the first month of launch.
